message:
fix(channel,sync): handle poisoned mutexes gracefully in Drop paths to prevent double-panic aborts

Two independent Drop-path methods used .expect() / .lock().expect() on
mutexes that could be poisoned if a prior panic occurred during unwinding.
A second panic from Drop triggers a process abort (Rust's double-panic
semantics), making debugging impossible and producing silent crashes in
production.

Broadcast channel — clear_waiter_registration (Recv::drop):
- Replaced `.lock().expect("broadcast lock poisoned")` with
  `let Ok(mut inner) = ... else { return; }` pattern
- When the mutex is poisoned during unwinding, we now bail out instead of
  panicking, accepting that the waiter slot may leak (harmless: channel is
  being torn down anyway)
- Added recv_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic regression test that
  poisons the mutex via catch_unwind then verifies Drop completes without
  abort
- Cleaned up existing test assertions: simplified verbose assert_with_log
  macros to standard assert! where the custom logging adds no value,
  reduced lock scope in total_sent and permit_send tests by extracting
  values before assertions

Resource pool — notify_return_wakers (PooledResource::drop):
- Same pattern: replaced .lock().expect("return_wakers lock") with
  let Ok(mut wakers) = ... else { return; }
- Prevents abort when a pool resource is dropped during panic unwinding
  while another thread previously poisoned the wakers mutex
- Moved fn assert_send<T: Send>() {} helper above init_test() call for
  consistent declaration-before-use ordering in tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
